final_text:
The regex expression to match the last date in the format YYYY-MM-DD in lines containing an IPv4 address is:

```regex
(?=.*?\b(?:(?:25[0-5]|2[0-4][0-9]|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])\.){3}(?:25[0-5]|2[0-4][0-9]|1[0-9][0-9]|[1-9]?[0-9])\b)\b\d{4}-(?:0[1-9]|1[0-2])-(?:0[1-9]|[12][0-9]|3[01])\b(?!.*?\b\d{4}-(?:0[1-9]|1[0-2])-(?:0[1-9]|[12][0-9]|3[01])\b)
```

This regex uses a positive lookahead to ensure the line contains an IPv4 address, matches the date, and uses a negative lookahead to ensure that no subsequent date exists in the same line, effectively selecting only the last date. The `\b` anchors ensure that the matches are not part of larger alphanumeric strings.
